headline: Musk’s Terafab And Lace’s Atom Beams Test ASML’s Long-Term Edge
summary: Elon Musk is reportedly planning a large scale semiconductor fabrication project called Terafab that could require extensive access to leading edge lithography tools. Microsoft backed startup Lace is developing helium atom beam lithography, targeting chip features smaller than those produced by current equipment. These developments arrive at a time when ASML Holding (ENXTAM:ASML) maintains a central role in advanced chip manufacturing through its EUV systems. For investors tracking...

Trades occur in $25 million increments — $5 million for each firm

headline: Microsoft-backed startup raises $40 million for advanced chipmaking equipment tech
summary: Lace, a Norway-headquartered chipmaking equipment startup which is backed by Microsoft, has raised $40 million in funding to further develop a technology ‌that could enable significant advances in semiconductor design and manufacturing, the company said on Monday.  To make ‌cutting-edge chips, manufacturers such as Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co and Intel employ a process called lithography that uses light to draw complex circuits ​that form the foundation of advanced artificial intelligence chips.  Manufacturers use light-based lithography systems made by the Dutch company ASML - which dominates the market - as they race to shrink chip components and squeeze in more features to boost the computing horsepower on a limited area of silicon.

raw.headline: Microsoft-backed startup raises $40 million for advanced chipmaking equipment tech
raw.summary: Lace, a Norway-headquartered chipmaking equipment startup which is backed by Microsoft, has raised $40 million in funding to further develop a technology ‌that could enable significant advances in semiconductor design and manufacturing, the company said on Monday.  To make ‌cutting-edge chips, manufacturers such as Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co and Intel employ a process called lithography that uses light to draw complex circuits ​that form the foundation of advanced artificial intelligence chips.  Manufacturers use light-based lithography systems made by the Dutch company ASML - which dominates the market - as they race to shrink chip components and squeeze in more features to boost the computing horsepower on a limited area of silicon.

headline: How to Win the Loser’s Game
summary: How good do you think your chances are of guessing the best-performing stock of the next 100 years?  The winning stock might not exist yet: Today’s superstars like  Nvidia  Apple  Microsoft  and leading consumer brands like  Nike  McDonald’  and  Walmart  weren’t even a twinkle in their founders’ eyes in 1926.  One dollar invested 100 years ago in cigarette maker Philip Morris (now  Altria  ) turned into $4.42 million by the end of last year, including dividends and spinoffs.

summary: The iShares Global 100 ETF is heavily concentrated in top US tech stocks, with 50% of its assets in its top 7 holdings. Read my analysis on IOO here.

headline: Microsoft’s Expanding AI Security Partnerships Deepen Azure And Copilot Workflows
summary: Microsoft (NasdaqGS:MSFT) is expanding AI driven security and automation partnerships across its ecosystem, centered on Azure and Copilot. Recent agreements span cybersecurity and automation with Accenture and UiPath, enterprise planning with Lumel, and proposal automation with Expedience. Additional collaborations with DataBahn and Opsera focus on cloud data and DevOps optimization across major enterprise environments. These partnerships extend into mission critical and regulated sectors,...
